question: Name the major battlefronts of World War II and provide a brief overview of the key events in each.
answer:
answer:
Western Front: Key events include the German invasion of France (1940), the Battle of Britain (1940), the Allied D-Day landings in Normandy (1944), and the Battle of the Bulge (1944-1945); Eastern Front: Characterized by Operation Barbarossa (1941), the Battle of Stalingrad (1942-1943), the Battle of Kursk (1943), and the final Soviet push to the Battle of Berlin (1945); Pacific Theatre: Began with the attack on Pearl Harbor (1941), with turning points at the Battle of Midway (1942) and the Guadalcanal Campaign (1942-1943), followed by the Allied island-hopping strategy and culminating in the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki (1945); Mediterranean and North African Theatre: Included the North African Campaign with the pivotal Battle of El Alamein (1942), followed by the Allied invasions of Sicily and Italy (1943); China Burma India Theatre: Encompassed the ongoing Second Sino-Japanese War and the Burma Campaign (1942-1945), where Allied forces fought to secure supply lines to China; Battle of the Atlantic: A continuous naval campaign (1939-1945) where German U-boats targeted Allied convoys, with the Allies gaining control through code-breaking and improved tactics.
